key_insight: A two-stage diffusion inversion pipeline can recover fine cloud microstructures from low-resolution multispectral satellite imagery with 4× super-resolution.
engineering_relevance: This improves the fidelity of cloud microphysics observations, which can support better weather-modification decisions such as cloud seeding and broader climate analysis.
method: Stage 1: train on real paired low/high-resolution data to learn sensor degradation and alignment.
Stage 2: self-supervised internal downgrading of high-resolution data to refine structures and synthesize realistic textures.
Use cascaded diffusion inversion to reconstruct high-frequency cloud details from low-resolution inputs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the method outperforms transformer-based and diffusion-based baselines on reconstruction accuracy and visual quality, with ablations showing Stage 1 helps coarse fidelity and Stage 2 adds detail.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of paired multispectral satellite data, and generalization across sensors or atmospheric conditions should be validated.
paper_title: Recovering Cloud Microstructures with Cascaded Diffusion Inversion
